sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: Treasury withdrawal to fund 12 months of Hardware Wallet Maintenance 2026 for Cardano hardware-wallet support, compatibility, tooling, integration support, release support, and vendor-required audits.
Recipient: Intersect 2026 Treasury Reserve Smart Contract stake address stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v

Deliverables: Ledger and Trezor compatibility updates, Maintenance of supporting interoperability libraries and cardano-hw-cli, Developer support for ecosystem integrators, Support for integration paths involving externally maintained components where shared hardware-wallet flows intersect, Vendor-required product or security audits where firmware or app changes trigger them, Transparent capped time-and-materials reporting, Milestone-based disbursement controls

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 1,310,960 ada, consisting of 1,272,777 ada for Cardano Hardware Wallet Maintenance & Compatibility Assurance and 38,183 ada for the Intersect Budget Administration fee.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposed scope is limited to maintaining hardware-wallet compatibility, security, supporting libraries and tooling, release support, and vendor-required audit work, and is not intended to build a new wallet product.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Continuous maintenance is needed because Cardano protocol evolution, Ledger and Trezor release cycles, and wallet and dApp integration demand create compatibility risk for a security-critical access layer.

Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposer claims funding continuity in 2026 is less costly and less disruptive than waiting for breakage to accumulate and responding after users or integrators lose access.

Claim (governance, independently_verifiable, medium materiality): The proposer states that the proposal achieved the required 67% support threshold during the 2026 Intersect Budget Process Hydra Voting phase.

Claim (governance, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposer states that the withdrawal does not, at submission, breach the applicable 350M ada Net Change Limit covering Epoch 613 to Epoch 713.
missing: Independent evidence for: The proposed scope is limited to maintaining hardware-wallet compatibility, security, supporting libraries and tooling, release support, and vendor-required audit work, and is not intended to build a new wallet product.

Independent evidence for: Continuous maintenance is needed because Cardano protocol evolution, Ledger and Trezor release cycles, and wallet and dApp integration demand create compatibility risk for a security-critical access layer.

Independent evidence for: The proposer claims funding continuity in 2026 is less costly and less disruptive than waiting for breakage to accumulate and responding after users or integrators lose access.
Independent evidence for: The proposer claims the work aligns with Cardano strategic pillars for security, interoperability, developer experience, adoption, and secure user experience.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unknown (stale snapshot)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 1,310,960 ada, consisting of 1,272,777 ada for Cardano Hardware Wallet Maintenance & Compatibility Assurance and 38,183 ada for the Intersect Budget Administration fee." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposed scope is limited to maintaining hardware-wallet compatibility, security, supporting libraries and tooling, release support, and vendor-required audit work, and is not intended to build a new wallet product." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
